headlines.title: China's Gasoline Consumption Could Plunge 5.5% in 2026 as Oil Prices Surge
headlines.summary: China's gasoline demand has dropped since the Iran war upended the global oil markets and is on track to decline more than previously expected this year, due to the higher prices and the continued push toward electric vehicles. headlines.summary: Chinese state oil giant Sinopec is opening a major ultra-deep shale gas find after obtaining official government approval for proven geological reserves of 235.687 billion cubic meters in the Ziyang Dongfeng field in the Sichuan province. headlines.title: Global Oil Stockpiles Plunge as Iran War Chokes Supply
headlines.summary: The West’s international energy watchdog has warned that oil stockpiles were being drained at a record rate last month as the US’s war in Iran continues to choke supply in an “unprecedented” supply shock. headlines.title: China Still Cautious on Fuel Shipments Despite Eased Export Rules
headlines.summary: Despite the easing of the Chinese restrictions on fuel exports, shipments so far in May have been nearly half the volumes from before the Iran war, the Financial Times reports, citing data from Kpler. headlines.title: India's Wholesale Inflation Hits 3.5-Year High as Fuel Costs Surge 25%
headlines.summary: Soaring energy costs sent India's wholesale inflation surging in April to the highest level in three and a half years and well above analyst estimates, as the worst oil supply shock in history is taking its toll on economies. headlines.title: Malaysia Warns of Surge in Iranian Ship-to-Ship Oil Transfers
headlines.summary: Iran’s dark fleet vessels have been increasingly engaged in ship-to-ship transfers just outside Malaysian waters, exploiting gaps in jurisdiction and enforcement rules to continue shipping oil to China, according to the Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency. headlines.title: Japan-Bound Oil Tanker Clears Strait of Hormuz Amid Iranian Crackdown
headlines.summary: A tanker carrying crude oil to Japan has passed through the Strait of Hormuz, Reuters reported today, citing data from LSEG.
